steps.title: Identify the key contrast signal
steps.content: The text states the data "were mostly inaccessible until a recent data-transfer project made them ______." The word "until" signals a reversal: the data went from inaccessible to their opposite state. The blank must name that opposite state.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'predictable': means able to be known in advance; a data-transfer project moves files, not forecasts their behavior, so this does not fit the inaccessible-to-usable reversal. ✗
  • Choice B — 'complicated': means complex or difficult to understand; this contradicts the idea that the project helped a researcher actually use the data. ✗
  • Choice C — 'representative': means standing as a typical example or symbol; the text focuses on the data becoming usable, not on whether they are typical of anything. ✗
  • Choice D — 'available': means accessible and ready to be used; this is the direct opposite of "inaccessible" and explains how the researcher could make use of the information. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
